_readme: Worked example of the tagging cache, pre-filled for the fabricated demo book so
`python scripts/analyze.py --source demo` renders cleanly out of the box. Copy this
to tags.json (gitignored) as a starting point, or let Claude build tags.json for your
real holdings during a run. Two parts:
taxonomy: the buckets your exposure chart splits into. Keep it small (aim <=8), give
each a human label + color + one-line desc, and always keep 'other'.
holdings: ticker -> {tag, asset_class, confidence, note}. 'tag' must be a taxonomy id.
overrides: same shape as holdings; these always win (use for hand-corrections).
Resolution order per ticker: overrides > holdings > 'other'. Single names get a leaf
bucket; basket ETFs/funds get the parent theme only (don't force a mixed ETF into a leaf).
The taxonomy below is one example (an AI-buildout thesis). Yours can be anything:
dividend income, sector rotation, factor sleeves, whatever you actually invest around.

taxonomy.label: AI picks & shovels
taxonomy.desc: Self-funded semis / power / infra incumbents. The crowded rotation; carries the capex-blink risk.

taxonomy.label: AI debt-funded infra
taxonomy.desc: Neoclouds / miners-to-HPC / single-customer infra. Highest torque up, first to break if financing tightens.

taxonomy.label: Megacap AI
taxonomy.desc: Hyperscaler spenders funding the buildout from cash flow. Earnings risk, not a multiple crash.

taxonomy.label: AI software
taxonomy.desc: Application / SaaS applying AI to an existing book. Execution risk, not a capex cliff.

taxonomy.label: AI thematic ETF / fund
taxonomy.desc: Diversified AI baskets and funds. Tagged at theme level only (mixed internals not split).

taxonomy.label: Non-AI tech
taxonomy.desc: Tech / internet names outside the AI-capex thesis (fintech, consumer, insurtech).

taxonomy.label: Broad market
taxonomy.desc: Diversified index / dividend ETFs. Diversification, not concentration.

taxonomy.label: Other
taxonomy.desc: Doesn't fit the thesis (non-thesis single names, sector ETFs, hedges), or not yet classified.
